method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/ccrb (Akamai + nginx + AWS ALB + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace), the online complaint intake (.../file-a-complaint/file-online.page), and the Complaint Status Lookup app (apps.nyc.gov/ccrb-status-lookup). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Civilian Complaint Review Board (CCRB)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 4 assets with column schemas; noted they are daily-updated, automated mirrors of the CCRB Complaints Database behind the Data Transparency Initiative dashboards.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' shared platform (Akamai/nginx/AWS ALB/Dynatrace) + a Complaint Status Lookup app on apps.nyc.gov; data via NYC Open Data (Socrata) and in-house Data Transparency Initiative dashboards
meta.netnew: MisconductComplaint — file a police-misconduct complaint against an officer via API (today an online web form only)
meta.tagline: CCRB's accountability data is a national transparency model, but it ships as dashboards and CSV with no API, and the complaint it exists to intake is a web form.
meta.headline: Expose CCRB's model-grade, daily-updated misconduct data as one queryable, agent-native contract — and give the act of filing a complaint the API it has never had.

headline_findings: CCRB is a police-oversight transparency model whose data is generously open but delivered ONLY as Data Transparency Initiative dashboards and four daily-updated Socrata datasets (complaints, allegations, officers, penalties) — there is no API.

The corpus is a tightly-normalized accountability model keyed on Complaint Id and Tax ID: officer-level Total/Substantiated complaint counts, per-allegation FADO records with CCRB-vs-NYPD dispositions, and the APU-vs-NYPD penalty gap.

The two acts a member of the public performs — filing a misconduct complaint (.../file-online) and checking its status (apps.nyc.gov/ccrb-status-lookup) — are disconnected JavaScript screens with no machine-readable contract.
Net-new write surface: file a MisconductComplaint against an officer — the reason the agency exists — has no API and no Open Data twin. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.
The gap is delivery, not openness: the record is model-grade, but it is not a queryable, agent-native API, and intake is not connected to it.

apis_observed.note: 4 CCRB datasets (2fir-qns4, 6xgr-kwjq, 2mby-ccnw, keep-pkmh); SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints, updated daily and automated. The read side of the CCRB Complaints Database — the one real, open CCRB API.
File a misconduct complaint online; server-rendered JavaScript page, no JSON/OpenAPI. The net-new write gap.
Check the status of a filed complaint; standalone screen disconnected from intake, no machine-readable contract.

About, complaint process, policy, and the Data Transparency Initiative dashboards (complaints/allegations/members-of-service/victims/feedback). Akamai edge, nginx, AWS ALB, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
Alternate complaint channel referenced from CCRB's file-a-complaint pages.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite (Akamai/nginx/AWS ALB/Dynatrace) + a standalone status-lookup app; modern managed hosting but generic and API-less.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content or site API; the informational site and DTI dashboards expose HTML/JS only.
scorecard_notes.openData: National model — 4 daily-updated, automated, disaggregated Socrata datasets plus interactive DTI dashboards.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core objects (Complaint, Allegation, PoliceOfficer, Penalty) richly modeled and officer-level; CCRB-vs-NYPD disposition and penalty gaps preserved.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: Filing a complaint exists as an online web form + 311 + mail, but there is no intake API and status is a disconnected screen.
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native surface, no queryable API over the open data.
scorecard_notes.ownership: CCRB owns and actively drives its own transparency (DTI) and the intake/status apps, but rides a citywide shared platform and exposes no API of its own.

fruit.name: Complaints Against Police Officers
fruit.notes: The incident record. Keyed on Complaint Id; carries CCRB Complaint Disposition, reason for contact, outcome of encounter, borough/precinct/location type, and Video/BWC evidence flags.

fruit.name: Allegations Against Police Officers
fruit.notes: Finest grain — one row per allegation per officer. FADO Type, specific allegation, CCRB vs NYPD disposition, officer tenure/rank/command, victim race/gender/age range. Joins on Complaint Id and Tax ID.

fruit.name: Police Officers (accused / members of service)
fruit.notes: The accused-officer roll keyed on Tax ID: rank, command, race/gender, active status, and the distinctive Total Complaints / Total Substantiated Complaints counts per officer.

fruit.name: Penalties (discipline outcomes)
fruit.notes: The APU (Administrative Prosecution Unit) trial track and the NYPD's final penalty — Board recommendation vs APU trial/plea vs NYPD Officer Penalty. Joins on Complaint Id and Tax ID.

fruit.name: Data Transparency Initiative dashboards
fruit.notes: Model-grade transparency rendered as HTML/JS dashboards (complaints, allegations, members of service, victims/alleged, feedback) over the CCRB Complaints Database. Same corpus as Open Data, but not itself an API.

fruit.name: Check complaint status
fruit.notes: Standalone status-lookup screen on apps.nyc.gov; read-only UI, no API, not joined back to the record. The read half of the intake gap.

fruit.name: File a misconduct complaint online
fruit.notes: The single most important civic act CCRB exists for — file a complaint of NYPD misconduct against an officer. A JavaScript web form (or 311 / mail / in person); no API and no Open Data twin. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.
